description: A single-threaded reference-counting pointer. Multiple owners of the same data on the heap. Dropped when the last Rc goes out of scope. Python equivalent: default object semantics (reference counting under the hood).

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Increments the reference count and returns a new Rc pointing to the same data.
methods.example:
let a = Rc::new(5);
let b = Rc::clone(&a);

methods.name: strong_count
methods.description: Returns the number of strong (owning) pointers to this allocation.

methods.name: try_unwrap
methods.description: Returns the inner value if the Rc has exactly one strong reference.

methods.name: downgrade
methods.description: Creates a Weak pointer that does not prevent deallocation.
